evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useContentResponsive.ts` is packaged as a content-area hook but only tracks `window.innerWidth/innerHeight`; meanwhile `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useContentResponsive.test.ts` still mocks `PanesContext` and asserts context-driven behavior, indicating boundary/ownership drift between shared hook and layout context.

`src/shared/lib/NetworkStatusManager.ts` combines a browser lifecycle singleton, app-level initialization behavior, and DEV-only window debug/simulation globals in one `shared/lib` module, while `src/shared/lib/ProjectScopedCache.ts` is a pure generic utility; this co-location blurs what `shared/lib` is supposed to own.

findings.summary: Responsive hook ownership is unclear: viewport-only implementation vs context-coupled test contract
evidence: `useContentResponsive` computes breakpoints from local viewport state and never imports/uses `PanesContext`.
Its test suite mocks `@/shared/contexts/PanesContext` and expects `contentBreakpoints` passthrough behavior, which no longer matches the module boundary implemented in the hook.
findings.suggestion: Choose one boundary and enforce it consistently: either (a) keep this as a pure viewport hook and remove PanesContext-based tests/docs, or (b) move context-aware logic into this hook (or a dedicated `usePaneContentResponsive`) and keep viewport fallback explicit. Update file name and test placement to reflect the chosen owner.

findings.summary: `shared/lib` mixes pure utilities with app-runtime services and debug wiring
evidence: `ProjectScopedCache` is a pure reusable data structure with no runtime side effects.

`NetworkStatusManager` performs browser event wiring, singleton lifecycle initialization, and DEV-only `window` debug/simulation exports (`__NETWORK_STATUS_MANAGER__`, `simulateNetworkChange`) inside the same module.
findings.suggestion: Split runtime service concerns from utility library concerns: move `NetworkStatusManager` to a service-oriented package (for example `src/shared/services/network/`) and move debug helpers to a separate debug module loaded only in DEV. Keep `shared/lib` for side-effect-free primitives like `ProjectScopedCache`.
